message:
feat(watch): add stale detection system for watch daemon (Issue #54)

The watch daemon can enter a stuck state where it runs but fails to
index new conversations due to connector parsing issues or similar
problems. This commit adds automatic stale detection with configurable
recovery actions.

New types:
- StaleAction: enum for recovery behavior (Warn, Rebuild, None)
- StaleConfig: configuration loaded from environment variables
- StaleDetector: monitors ingest activity and detects stuck states

Configuration via environment variables:
- CASS_WATCH_STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS: hours without ingests before stale (default: 24)
- CASS_WATCH_STALE_ACTION: warn|rebuild|none (default: warn)
- CASS_WATCH_STALE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MINS: check frequency (default: 60)
- CASS_WATCH_STALE_MIN_ZERO_SCANS: consecutive empty scans threshold (default: 10)

Detection logic:
- Tracks last successful ingest timestamp (>0 conversations)
- Counts consecutive zero-conversation scans
- Triggers action when both thresholds exceeded
- Avoids log spam by tracking warning emission state

Recovery actions:
- Warn: log warning but continue (default, safe)
- Rebuild: automatically trigger full index rebuild
- None: disable stale detection entirely

Also syncs bead tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1m6m","title":"[EPIC] HTML Session Export: Beautiful, Encrypted, Cross-Platform","description":"# Epic: Premium HTML Session Export

## Overview
Create a world-class feature for exporting coding agent sessions as beautifully formatted, 
self-contained static HTML files. This feature provides both TUI and robot mode access,
with optional password-based encryption using Web Crypto API for client-side decryption.

## Strategic Goals
1. **Knowledge Preservation**: Allow users to archive and share valuable coding sessions
2. **Cross-Agent Value**: Export sessions from ANY agent (Claude, Codex, Cursor, etc.)
3. **Security-First**: Optional encryption ensures sensitive content stays protected
4. **Universal Access**: Files work offline, on any device, with or without internet
5. **Stripe-Level UX**: Premium visual design matching cass's TUI quality standards

## Key Features
- Self-contained HTML files with embedded CSS/JS
- Progressive enhancement: works offline, enhanced with CDN (Tailwind, Prism.js)
- Optional AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation
- Cross-platform download folder detection (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Smart file naming: agent_project_datetime_topic.html
- Beautiful conversation rendering with syntax highlighting
- Dark/light mode toggle in exported file
- Print-friendly styles for PDF export from browser
- Search within exported conversation
- Collapsible tool calls and code blocks
- Full TUI modal with preview and options
- Complete robot mode API for automation

## Technical Approach
- Rust-side: HTML template generation with minified inline CSS/JS
- Encryption: PBKDF2 (WebCrypto-compatible) + AES-GCM with explicit salt/iv/iterations
- Web Crypto: PBKDF2 for key derivation (client-side compatible)
- File naming: Sanitize and slugify with chrono timestamps
- Downloads: dirs::download_dir() with fallback strategies

## Research Sources
- StatiCrypt: https://github.com/robinmoisson/staticrypt (static HTML encryption patterns)
- Web Crypto API: https://gist.github.com/chrisveness/43bcda93af9f646d083fad678071b90a
- Tailwind Chat UI: https://flowbite.com/docs/components/chat-bubble/
- Prism.js CDN: https://prismjs.com/ (syntax highlighting)
- Print CSS: https://print-css.rocks/ (print-friendly styles)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Export works for all 11 supported agents
- [ ] File opens correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Encryption/decryption works in all browsers
- [ ] Offline mode displays conversation correctly
- [ ] Print to PDF produces readable output
- [ ] TUI modal is intuitive and polished
- [ ] Robot mode provides complete API coverage","notes":"### QA Gate (must satisfy before closing)
- Unit tests for renderer, encryption, filename, JS/CSS injection, and CLI arg parsing.
- Integration tests for full export pipeline + log verification.
- E2E browser tests across Chrome/Firefox/Safari (or WebKit) with detailed logs and metrics.
- Publish a consolidated test report (JSON + markdown) with version, data hash, and environment.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:26:49.360766997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T05:00:37.131138616Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T05:00:37.131119921Z","close_reason":"All 16 subtasks completed. HTML export feature fully implemented with: core rendering, encryption (AES-256-GCM), CDN fallback, print styles, TUI modal, robot API, test fixtures, logging, unit tests, integration tests, and E2E browser tests (171 passing across Chrome/Firefox/Safari). Acceptance criteria verified through comprehensive test  {"id":"bd-1ueu","title":"Perf: lazy SQLite open to cut startup cost","description":"# Perf: Lazy SQLite Open to Cut Startup Cost

## Goal
Delay opening SQLite until a command actually needs it, reducing CLI/TUI startup time.

## Strategy
- Introduce a lazy initializer for DB pool/connection.
- Keep config parsing and help paths DB-free.
- Ensure thread-safety and single initialization.

## Logging
- Startup timing logs: pre-init, post-init, and reason for DB open.

## Tests
- Unit: lazy init only when DB functions are called; concurrent callers share one open.
- Integration: commands that should not touch DB (help/health) do not open it.
- E2E: startup benchmark script logs cold start times before/after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Measurable startup reduction (record ms).
- No regressions in DB-required commands.
- Logs include open reason and duration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:20:35.430011738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735628215Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735384155Z","close_reason":"Implemented LazyDb struct in storage/sqlite.rs with parking_lot::Mutex<Option<Connection>>. Updated run_stats, run_context, run_timeline, state_meta_json to use LazyDb. Added lazy_db_to_cli_error helper. 5 unit tests pass. cargo check, clippy, fmt all clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-22k2","title":"P6.14a: Audit remaining mocks/fakes and coverage gaps","description":"# P6.14a: Audit remaining mocks/fakes and coverage gaps

## Goal
Produce an explicit, up-to-date inventory of all mock/fake/stub usage in `src/`, `tests/`, and `scripts/`, plus a gap map for no-mock unit and E2E coverage.

## Why
The repo still contains explicit mock/fake code paths (e.g., MockDaemon in `src/search/daemon_client.rs`, fake binaries in `tests/e2e_install_easy.rs`, fake model file in `tests/semantic_integration.rs`, mock probe helpers in `src/sources/index.rs`). We need a definitive list before removal.

## Tasks
1. `rg -n "Mock|mock|fake|Fake|stub|Stub" -S src tests scripts` and capture all hits.
2. Classify each hit as one of: (a) remove/replace, (b) convert to real fixture, (c) allowlist (true OS/hardware boundary).
3. Map each hit to a downstream task (daemon, sources, install, UI/TUI, semantic model, connectors, CI gating).
4. Identify E2E gaps: flows without script coverage or missing structured logs (e.g., no `scripts/tests/run_all.sh`).

## Deliverable
- `test-results/no_mock_audit.md` with:
  - full hit list + file references
  - classification/decision
  - planned replacement strategy
  - dependencies per subsystem

## Acceptance Criteria
- Audit report exists and is referenced by all follow-up tasks.
- Every mock/fake usage is assigned to a replacement or allowlist path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:17.295118211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T06:27:48.197521340Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T06:27:48.197458602Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive audit report created at test-results/no_mock_audit.md. Cataloged 814 mock/fake/stub hits across 35 files. Classified each as REMOVE/REPLACE, CONVERT TO FIXTURE, or ALLOWLIST. Mapped all to downstream tasks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-24uo","title":"Implement robot mode API for HTML export automation","description":"# Task: Robot Mode API for HTML Export

## Context
Provide a complete CLI API for automated HTML export, enabling AI agents and
scripts to programmatically export sessions without TUI interaction.

## Command Design

### Primary Command
```bash
cass export-html <session_path> [OPTIONS]
```

### Full Flag Reference
```
cass export-html <session_path>
    --robot                     # JSON output (required for automation)
    --output-dir <path>         # Override output directory (default: downloads)
    --filename <name>           # Override filename (default: auto-generated)
    --encrypt                   # Enable password encryption
    --password <pwd>            # Password (required if --encrypt)
    --password-stdin            # Read password from stdin (secure)
    --include-tools             # Include tool calls in export
    --collapse-threshold <n>    # Collapse messages longer than n chars
    --no-cdns                   # Disable CDN references (fully offline)
    --theme <dark|light>        # Default theme
    --dry-run                   # Validate without writing file
    --explain                   # Show export plan without executing
```

### Batch Export
```bash
cass export-html-batch [OPTIONS]
    --sessions <file>           # File with session paths (one per line)
    --sessions-from -           # Read from stdin
    --query <search>            # Export sessions matching query
    --limit <n>                 # Max sessions to export
    --robot                     # JSON output
    --output-dir <path>         # Output directory for all files
    --encrypt                   # Encrypt all exports
    --password <pwd>            # Shared password for all
```

## JSON Output Schema

### Success Response
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "exported": {
        "session_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
        "output_path": "/home/user/Downloads/claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "filename": "claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "size_bytes": 87654,
        "encrypted": true,
        "messages_count": 42,
        "agent": "claude_code",
        "workspace": "/projects/myapp",
        "title": "Fix authentication bug"
    },
    "_meta": {
        "elapsed_ms": 234,
        "cass_version": "0.5.0"
    }
}
```

### Batch Success Response
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "exported": [
        { /* session 1 */ },
        { /* session 2 */ }
    ],
    "failed": [
        {
            "session_path": "/path/to/broken.jsonl",
            "error": "Session not found"
        }
    ],
    "summary": {
        "total": 5,
        "exported": 4,
        "failed": 1,
        "total_size_bytes": 456789
    },
    "_meta": {
        "elapsed_ms": 1234
    }
}
```

### Error Response
```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "code": 3,
        "kind": "session_not_found",
        "message": "Session file does not exist",
        "hint": "Verify the path or use 'cass search' to find sessions",
        "retryable": false
    }
}
```

## Exit Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | Success |
| 1 | General failure |
| 2 | Usage/argument error |
| 3 | Session not found |
| 4 | Output directory not writable |
| 5 | Encryption error |
| 6 | Password required but not provided |

## Implementation

### CLI Parser (extend Commands enum in lib.rs)
```rust
#[derive(Subcommand)]
enum Commands {
    // ... existing commands ...
    
    /// Export session as beautiful HTML file
    #[command(name = "export-html")]
    ExportHtml {
        /// Path to session file
        session: PathBuf,
        
        /// Output directory (default: downloads folder)
        #[arg(long)]
        output_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
        
        /// Custom filename (default: auto-generated)
        #[arg(long)]
        filename: Option<String>,
        
        /// Enable password encryption
        #[arg(long)]
        encrypt: bool,
        
        /// Password for encryption
        #[arg(long)]
        password: Option<String>,
        
        /// Read password from stdin
        #[arg(long)]
        password_stdin: bool,
        
        /// Include tool calls in export
        #[arg(long, default_value_t = true)]
        include_tools: bool,
        
        /// Collapse messages longer than N characters
        #[arg(long)]
        collapse_threshold: Option<usize>,
        
        /// Disable CDN references
        #[arg(long)]
        no_cdns: bool,
        
        /// Default theme
        #[arg(long, default_value = "dark")]
        theme: String,
        
        /// Validate without writing
        #[arg(long)]
        dry_run: bool,
        
        /// Show export plan
        #[arg(long)]
        explain: bool,
    },
    
    /// Batch export multiple sessions
    #[command(name = "export-html-batch")]
    ExportHtmlBatch {
        /// File with session paths
        #[arg(long)]
        sessions: Option<PathBuf>,
        
        /// Read session paths from stdin
        #[arg(long)]
        sessions_from_stdin: bool,
        
        /// Export sessions matching query
        #[arg(long)]
        query: Option<String>,
        
        /// Max sessions to export
        #[arg(long)]
        limit: Option<usize>,
        
        // ... shared options ...
    },
}
```

### Password Handling
```rust
fn get_password(
    password_arg: Option<&str>,
    password_stdin: bool,
    encrypt: bool,
) -> Result<Option<String>, CliError> {
    if !encrypt {
        return Ok(None);
    }
    
    // Priority: explicit arg > stdin > error
    if let Some(pwd) = password_arg {
        return Ok(Some(pwd.to_string()));
    }
    
    if password_stdin {
        let mut pwd = String::new();
        std::io::stdin().read_line(&mut pwd)?;
        return Ok(Some(pwd.trim().to_string()));
    }
    
    Err(CliError::new(6, "password_required", 
        "Password required for encryption", 
        Some("Use --password or --password-stdin")))
}
```

### Explain Mode Output
```json
{
    "plan": {
        "session_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
        "agent": "claude_code",
        "messages": 42,
        "output_path": "/home/user/Downloads/claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "estimated_size_bytes": 87000,
        "options": {
            "encrypted": true,
            "include_tools": true,
            "collapse_threshold": null,
            "cdns_enabled": true,
            "default_theme": "dark"
        }
    },
    "warnings": [
        "Session contains 3 very long messages (>10KB)"
    ]
}
```

## Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_export_html_basic() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html", 
        "fixtures/sample_session.jsonl",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.success);
    assert!(result.exported.output_path.exists());
}

#[test]
fn test_export_html_encrypted() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html",
        "fixtures/sample_session.jsonl",
        "--encrypt",
        "--password", "test123",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.exported.encrypted);
}

#[test]
fn test_export_html_batch() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html-batch",
        "--query", "authentication",
        "--limit", "5",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.summary.total <= 5);
}
```

## Documentation
Add to `cass robot-docs` system:
- `cass robot-docs export-html` - Full API reference
- Examples in robot-docs examples topic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single session export works with all flags
- [ ] Batch export handles multiple sessions
- [ ] Password can be provided via arg or stdin
- [ ] JSON output is well-structured and parseable
- [ ] Exit codes are correct for each error type
- [ ] Dry-run mode works correctly
- [ ] Explain mode shows plan without executing","notes":"### Testing & Logging
- Unit: JSON schema for --robot output; errors include machine-readable codes.
- Integration: CLI --robot export against fixtures verifies output path + logs array.
- E2E: script validates jq parsing and log bundle (command, args, exit code).
- Logging: ensure export_id correlates logs across stages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:30:31.220926664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:13:17.090670154Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:13:17.090583862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["html-export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-2r58","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:35:12.709271282Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-8w6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T21:39:08.612381072Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-u4me","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:35:20.479079969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28iz","title":"P6.14b: No-mock policy + allowlist + CI gate","description":"# P6.14b: No-mock policy + allowlist + CI gate

## Goal
Define and enforce a strict no-mock policy for tests, with an explicit allowlist for unavoidable OS/hardware boundaries, and CI enforcement to prevent regressions.

## Why
Without a policy, mocks/fakes will creep back in. CI should fail fast on new mock/fake usage unless explicitly approved.

## Scope
- Policy definition (what is allowed, what is forbidden, how to request an exception).
- Allowlist format (file path + rationale + expiration date).
- CI gate: fail on new Mock/fake/stub usage outside allowlist.

## Steps
1. Draft policy section in `TESTING.md` (no-mock definition + rationale).
2. Create allowlist file (e.g., `test-results/no_mock_allowlist.json`) with structured entries.
3. Add a CI check (e.g., `scripts/validate_ci.sh`) that:
   - runs `rg`/`ast-grep` over `src/` + `tests/`
   - diff against allowlist
   - emits a report with violations
4. Provide a local opt-out for developers (explicit env var), but keep CI strict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy section is explicit and discoverable.
- CI fails on new mock/fake usage without allowlist entry.
- Allowlist includes rationale + owner + review date.

## Dependencies
- Uses output from P6.14a audit to seed allowlist.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:30.928058148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T08:05:32.761866266Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T08:05:32.761662352Z","close_reason":"Completed: (1) TESTING.md created with no-mock policy, (2) scripts/validate_ci.sh CI gate working, (3) test-results/no_mock_allowlist.json with structured entries. All acceptance criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28iz","depends_on_id":"bd-22k2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:09:08.244308624Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hfc","title":"Unit tests for HTML export core functions","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for all HTML export core functionality with structured logging verification, covering template generation, encryption, filename generation, CSS rendering, and JavaScript functionality.

## Testing Framework Setup

### Dependencies
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
tracing-test = "*"
pretty_assertions = "*"
proptest = "*"
test-case = "*"
```

### Test Module Structure
```
tests/html_export/
├── mod.rs
├── template_tests.rs
├── encryption_tests.rs
├── filename_tests.rs
├── css_tests.rs
├── js_tests.rs
├── renderer_tests.rs
└── fixtures/
    ├── sample_session.jsonl
    ├── unicode_session.jsonl
    └── code_heavy_session.jsonl
```

## Template Generation Tests (template_tests.rs)

### HTML Structure Tests
```rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;
use pretty_assertions::assert_eq;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_template_generates_valid_html5_doctype() {
    let session = fixtures::minimal_session();
    let html = generate_html_template(&session, &ExportOptions::default());

    assert!(html.starts_with("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
        "HTML must start with DOCTYPE declaration");
    assert!(logs_contain("component=template operation=generate"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_template_includes_all_required_meta_tags() {
    let html = generate_html_template(&fixtures::minimal_session(), &default_opts());

    assert!(html.contains(r#"<meta charset="UTF-8">"#));
    assert!(html.contains(r#"<meta name="viewport""#));
    assert!(html.contains(r#"content="width=device-width"#));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_template_has_semantic_structure() {
    let html = generate_html_template(&fixtures::minimal_session(), &default_opts());

    // Must have proper semantic elements
    assert!(html.contains("<header"));
    assert!(html.contains("<main"));
    assert!(html.contains("<article"));
    assert!(html.contains("</html>"));

    // Verify nesting order in logs
    assert!(logs_contain("Semantic structure validated"));
}
```

### XSS Prevention Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_escape_html_script_tags() {
    let malicious = r#"<script>alert("xss")</script>"#;
    let escaped = escape_html(malicious);

    assert!(!escaped.contains("<script>"));
    assert!(escaped.contains("<script>"));
}

#[test]
fn test_escape_html_event_handlers() {
    let malicious = r#"<img onerror="alert(1)" src=x>"#;
    let escaped = escape_html(malicious);

    assert!(!escaped.contains("onerror="));
}

#[test_case(r#"<a href="javascript:alert(1)">click</a>"#; "javascript url")]
#[test_case(r#"<div onmouseover="evil()">hover</div>"#; "event handler")]
#[test_case(r#"<iframe src="data:text/html,<script>evil()</script>">"#; "data url")]
fn test_escape_html_prevents_xss_vectors(input: &str) {
    let escaped = escape_html(input);
    let html = format!("<div>{}</div>", escaped);

    // Verify no executable content
    assert!(!html.contains("javascript:"));
    assert!(!html.contains("onerror="));
    assert!(!html.contains("onmouseover="));
}

#[test]
fn test_escape_html_preserves_unicode() {
    let unicode = "Japanese chars and emoji";
    let escaped = escape_html(unicode);
    assert_eq!(escaped, unicode);
}
```

## Encryption Tests (encryption_tests.rs)

```rust
#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_encrypt_produces_valid_payload_structure() {
    let plaintext = "<html>test content</html>";
    let password = "secure-password-123";

    let payload = encrypt_for_html(plaintext, password).unwrap();

    // Verify structure
    assert!(payload.version >= 1);
    assert!(!payload.salt.is_empty());
    assert!(!payload.iv.is_empty());
    assert!(!payload.ciphertext.is_empty());
    assert!(!payload.tag.is_empty());
    assert!(payload.iterations >= 600_000);

    // Verify base64 encoding
    assert!(base64_decode(&payload.salt).is_ok());
    assert!(base64_decode(&payload.iv).is_ok());

    // Verify logging
    assert!(logs_contain("component=encryption operation=encrypt_payload"));
    assert!(logs_contain("Encryption complete"));
}

#[test]
fn test_encrypt_salt_is_random() {
    let password = "same-password";
    let plaintext = "same-content";

    let enc1 = encrypt_for_html(plaintext, password).unwrap();
    let enc2 = encrypt_for_html(plaintext, password).unwrap();

    // Salt must be different each time
    assert_ne!(enc1.salt, enc2.salt);
    assert_ne!(enc1.iv, enc2.iv);
    assert_ne!(enc1.ciphertext, enc2.ciphertext);
}

#[test]
fn test_encrypt_with_empty_password_fails() {
    let result = encrypt_for_html("content", "");
    assert!(result.is_err());
}

#[test]
fn test_encrypt_with_unicode_password() {
    let plaintext = "secret content";
    let password = "unicode-password-test";

    let result = encrypt_for_html(plaintext, password);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

## Filename Generation Tests (filename_tests.rs)

```rust
use proptest::prelude::*;

#[test]
fn test_filename_format_matches_spec() {
    let conv = fixtures::conversation_with(
        "claude_code",
        Some("/projects/myapp"),
        Some(1705334400000),
        Some("Fix authentication bug"),
    );

    let filename = generate_export_filename(&conv);

    assert!(filename.starts_with("claude_"));
    assert!(filename.contains("myapp_"));
    assert!(filename.contains("2024_01_15_"));
    assert!(filename.contains("fix_authentication"));
    assert!(filename.ends_with(".html"));
}

#[test]
fn test_filename_sanitizes_unsafe_chars() {
    let unsafe_title = "Path/With Bad Chars";
    let conv = fixtures::conversation_with_title(unsafe_title);

    let filename = generate_export_filename(&conv);

    assert!(!filename.contains('/'));
    assert!(!filename.contains('\\'));
}

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn test_filename_always_filesystem_safe(
        agent in "[a-zA-Z_]{1,20}",
        workspace in "[a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,30}",
        title in "[a-zA-Z0-9 ]{0,50}",
    ) {
        let conv = fixtures::conversation_with(&agent, Some(&workspace), None, Some(&title));
        let filename = generate_export_filename(&conv);

        prop_assert!(!filename.is_empty());
        prop_assert!(filename.len() <= 255);
        prop_assert!(filename.ends_with(".html"));
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_downloads_dir_returns_valid_path() {
    let path = get_downloads_dir();
    assert!(!path.to_string_lossy().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_unique_filename_avoids_collision() {
    let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
    let base = "test.html";

    std::fs::write(temp.path().join(base), "existing").unwrap();

    let unique = unique_filename(temp.path(), base);

    assert_ne!(unique.file_name().unwrap().to_str().unwrap(), base);
}
```

```rust
fn test_css_variables_defined() {

    assert!(css.contains("--text-primary:"));

    assert!(css.contains("@media print"));
    assert!(css.contains("page-break-inside: avoid"));
}

#[test]
fn test_css_size_under_budget() {
    let css = generate_critical_css();
    let minified = minify_css(&css);

    assert!(minified.len() < 15_000,
        "CSS size {} exceeds 15KB budget", minified.len());
}
```

## Renderer Tests (renderer_tests.rs)

```rust
#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_render_conversation_produces_valid_html() {
    let conv = fixtures::sample_conversation();
    let renderer = ConversationRenderer::default();

    let html = renderer.render(&conv);

    assert!(html.contains("<main"));
    assert!(html.contains("</main>"));

    assert!(logs_contain("Beginning conversation render"));
}

#[test]
fn test_render_all_message_roles() {
    let conv = fixtures::conversation_with_all_roles();
    let html = ConversationRenderer::default().render(&conv);

    assert!(html.contains("message-user"));
    assert!(html.contains("message-agent"));
    assert!(html.contains("message-tool"));
}

#[test]
fn test_render_code_blocks_with_language() {
    let msg = Message {
        content: "```rust\nfn main() {}\n```".to_string(),
        role: MessageRole::Agent,
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let html = render_message(&msg, &RenderOptions::default());

    assert!(html.contains("language-rust"));
    assert!(html.contains("fn main"));
}

#[test]
fn test_render_tool_calls_collapsible() {
    let conv = fixtures::conversation_with_tool_calls();
    let html = ConversationRenderer::default().render(&conv);

    assert!(html.contains("<details"));
    assert!(html.contains("<summary>"));
}
```
